title: leucas urticifolia
intro: Leucas urticifolia, also known as the stinging nettle tree, is a plant species belonging to the family Lamiaceae, characterized by its ability to cause skin irritation when touched due to the presence of tiny hairs on its leaves. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. Leucas urticifolia is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and it is also valued for its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world, including Asia and Africa.
